---
title: "Polysorbate 60 (INS 435)"
slug: polysorbate-60-ins-435
category: "Additives & Functional"
category-slug: additives-functional
keywords: ["Polysorbate 60", "INS 435", "Emulsifier", "Stabilizer", "Food Additive", "Processed Foods", "FMCG"]
description: "Polysorbate 60 (INS 435) is a synthetic emulsifier and stabilizer, chemically polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monostearate"
---

## History and Sourcing

[Polysorbate 60 (INS 435)](polysorbate-60-ins-435.qmd) is a synthetic emulsifier and stabilizer, chemically polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monostearate. It is derived from sorbitol, stearic acid, and ethylene oxide. Lacking traditional Indian culinary roots, its presence in India is tied to the modern processed food industry and its global acceptance as a food additive, regulated by FSSAI.

## Home Kitchen Use

Polysorbate 60 is not used in traditional Indian home cooking. Home kitchens rely on natural ingredients and traditional techniques for emulsification and stabilization, making this synthetic additive irrelevant to conventional culinary practices.

## Industrial Applications

In industrial applications, Polysorbate 60 functions as a critical emulsifier and stabilizer. It prevents phase separation in products like ice creams, ensuring a smooth texture by inhibiting ice crystal growth. In baked goods, it enhances dough workability, increases volume, and improves crumb structure. It is also used in whipped toppings, gravies, sauces, and confectionery to maintain consistency and extend shelf life.

## Distinctions and Common Confusion

Polysorbate 60 is a synthetic food additive, distinct from natural emulsifiers like lecithin. Consumers often confuse it with other polysorbates or general stabilizers; however, its specific chemical structure (monostearate) makes it particularly effective for oil-in-water emulsions. It is an approved additive, used within regulatory limits, and its function is to create stable, uniform food products, not to be mistaken for a traditional ingredient.
